title: Fluid dispensing device
abstract: According to one aspect, a fluid dispensing device comprises a housing and a fluid moving device associated with the housing. The housing includes an interior surface defining a passage extending at least between an emission end and at least one aperture configured to facilitate entry of a fluid into the passage. The housing further includes a mounting apparatus adjacent the aperture and adapted to receive a fluid source. The fluid moving device is operable to develop air flow in the passage, wherein the aperture is disposed downstream of the fluid moving device and upstream of the emission end.
claims:
Claims WE CLAIM: 1. A fluid dispensing device, comprising: a housing including an interior surface defining a passage extending at least between an emission end and at least one aperture configured to facilitate entry of a fluid into the passage, the device housing further including a mounting apparatus adjacent the aperture and adapted to receive a fluid source; and a fluid moving device associated with the housing and operable to develop air flow in the passage, wherein the aperture is disposed downstream of the fluid moving device and upstream of the emission end. 2. The fluid dispensing device of claim 1, wherein the fluid source comprises a source of pressurized fluid and is secured to the mounting apparatus. 3. The fluid dispensing device of claim 2, wherein the source of pressurized fluid is an aerosol spray container. 4. The fluid dispensing device of claim 1, wherein the interior surface of the device housing is substantially cylindrical. 5. The fluid dispensing device of claim 4, further comprising an actuator comprising a substantially plate-shaped plunger, the plunger extending at least partially through the interior surface and being substantially parallel to a central longitudinal axis of the interior surface. 6. The fluid dispensing device of claim 4, wherein the fluid moving device comprises a fan comprising a rotational axis substantially parallel to a longitudinal central axis of the interior surface of the device housing. 7. The fluid dispensing device of claim 1, wherein the interior surface of the device housing surrounds at least one vane disposed between the fluid moving device and the aperture. 8. The fluid dispensing device of claim 7, wherein the at least one vane comprises a first portion substantially parallel with a rotational axis of the fluid moving device and a second portion that is at least one of curved and disposed at a non-zero angle relative to the rotational axis. 9. The fluid dispensing device of claim 1, wherein the interior surface of the device housing encloses at least one guiding member disposed downstream of the fluid moving device, the guiding member comprising an outer surface having substantially frustoconical shape having a diameter that decreases toward the emission end. 10. The fluid dispensing device of claim 1, wherein the aperture is disposed at a bottom portion of the interior surface and the fluid dispensing device further comprises a shield section disposed adjacent the emission end of the interior surface wherein a top portion of the shield section comprises a protruding member. 1 1. The fluid dispensing device of claim 2, wherein the source is a container comprising: a container housing; a button atop the container housing; an overcap at least partially enclosing the button, the overcap comprising an orifice to allow flow of the fluid, an aperture for facilitating actuation of the button, and a projecting member; and a fitting member disposed at least partially circumferentially about the container. 12. The fluid dispensing device of claim 1 1, wherein the interior surface of the device housing is substantially cylindrical and the container housing comprises a substantially 

 cylindrical portion, wherein a central longitudinal axis of the substantially cylindrical portion is less than 45 degrees from perpendicular to the central longitudinal axis of the interior surface. 13. The fluid dispensing device of claim 11, wherein the interior surface of the device housing is substantially cylindrical, the orifice faces the emission end, and the projecting member faces a direction opposite the orifice. 14. The fluid dispensing device of claim 13, wherein the aperture of the overcap is longer in a direction parallel to the central longitudinal axis of the interior surface than in a direction perpendicular to the central longitudinal axis of the interior surface. 15. The fluid dispensing device of claim 1, further comprising at least one light source configured to provide light to an application region. 16. The fluid dispensing device of claim 1, further comprising a rechargeable battery at least partially enclosed by the device housing. 17. The fluid dispensing device of claim 1, further comprising a circuit board in communication with the fluid moving device and at least one light source. 18. A fluid dispensing device, comprising: a housing including an interior surface defining a passage extending at least between an emission end and at least one aperture configured to facilitate entry of a fluid into the passage; and a handle associated with the housing, wherein the handle is disposed above the interior surface and the aperture is disposed along a bottom portion of the interior surface. 19. The fluid dispensing device of claim 18, wherein the handle extends between a front of the fluid dispensing device and a rear of the fluid dispensing device. 20. The fluid dispensing device of claim 18, wherein an underside of the handle comprises a trigger. 21. The fluid dispensing device of claim 20, wherein a bottom surface of the trigger is disposed at a higher level in an actuated state than in an unactuated state. 22. The fluid dispensing device of claim 21, wherein actuation of the trigger causes actuation of at least one of a fluid moving device, a plunger, and a light source. 23. The fluid dispensing device of claim 22, wherein actuation of the trigger causes a trigger arm to move about a pivot such that an engaging member of the trigger arm moves downwards to exert a downward force on the plunger such that downward movement of the plunger causes a release of the fluid. 24. The fluid dispensing device of claim 23, wherein actuation of the trigger causes actuation of the fluid moving device before release of the fluid. 25. The fluid dispensing device of claim 18, wherein the fluid is at least one of an insect repellant, an insect killer, an animal repellant, an animal killer, a pest repellant, a pest killer, a herbicide, a fungicide, a mildewcide, an antibacterial formulation, a fertilizer, a liquid plant food, a coating to protect plants against winter wind burn, a barrier, a material that creates a mist, and a material that creates a fog. 26. A method of dispensing fluid, the method comprising the steps of: providing fluid to a passage defined by an interior surface of a device housing of a fluid dispensing device, the passage including an emission end and at least one aperture, the aperture being configured to receive the fluid from a source of fluid and the device housing further including a mounting apparatus adjacent the aperture; and operating a fluid moving device associated with the device housing, the fluid moving device being disposed upstream of the aperture and the emission end being disposed downstream of the aperture. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein the fluid dispensing device comprises a handle, the method further comprising holding the fluid dispensing device by the handle such that the handle is disposed above the interior surface when the fluid is being provided to the passage. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the handle comprises a trigger, the method further comprising actuating the trigger by applying a vertical force on the trigger. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein a plunger is disposed at least partially through the interior surface and is operably coupled to the trigger, wherein actuating the trigger causes downward movement of the plunger. 30. The method of claim 26, wherein providing air flow to the passage further comprises imparting a spinning motion to the air provided. 31. The method of claim 26, wherein the source of the fluid is a first container having a button atop a container housing, an overcap at least partially enclosing the button, the overcap having an orifice to allow flow of fluid, an aperture for facilitating actuation of the button, and a projecting member, the method further comprising: coupling the first container to the fluid dispensing device such that the orifice faces the emission end and the projecting member faces a direction opposite the orifice. 32. The method of claim 31, further comprising uncoupling the first container from the fluid dispensing device and coupling a second container to the fluid dispensing device. 33. A container for fluid, comprising: a container housing; a button atop the container housing; an overcap at least partially enclosing the button, the overcap comprising an orifice to allow flow of the fluid and an aperture for facilitating actuation of the button, the overcap further comprising a projecting member; and a fitting member disposed at least partially circumferentially about the container. 35. The container of claim 33, wherein the button and the overcap are rotatable about a central longitudinal axis of the container and the overcap is at least one of secured to and integral with the button. 34. The container of claim 33, wherein the fitting member includes at least one shoulder protruding in a direction perpendicular to a central longitudinal axis of the container and wherein the fitting member is at least one of secured to and integral with the housing of the container. 35. The container of claim 33 operably coupled to a mounting apparatus included in a device housing of a fluid dispensing device, the projecting member of the overcap being disposed in a recess in the device housing and the fitting member being a shoulder disposed on a shelf of the mounting apparatus to prevent downward movement of the container. 36. The apparatus of claim 35, wherein the fluid dispensing device comprises an actuator that includes a plunger, the plunger extending at least partially through an interior wall of the device housing and extending at least partially through the aperture of the overcap. 37. The apparatus of claim 35, wherein the orifice is oriented toward an emission end of the device housing when the projecting member is disposed in the recess. 38. An apparatus comprising the container of claim 33 in fluid communication with a nozzle, the container comprising an unpressurized fluid in fluid communication with the nozzle via a first inlet, the nozzle having a second inlet adapted to receive a pressurized fluid from a pump such that the pressurized fluid shears the unpressurized fluid.
